explanations.a: Values close to 1 indicate well-separated and compact clusters.
explanations.b: Good clustering usually lies around 0.3 to 0.5.
explanations.c: Poor clustering gives silhouette values near 0.
explanations.d: Overlapping clusters give low or negative scores.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Davies-Bouldin score

explanations.a: This is an indirect effect, not the main purpose.
explanations.b: It measures more than similarity; it evaluates clustering quality.
explanations.c: Davies-Bouldin score evaluates cluster compactness and separation.
explanations.d: Cohesion alone is not sufficient without separation.

questions.question: If we take age feature also, the clustering will be

explanations.a: Adding irrelevant features can reduce clustering quality.
explanations.b: Additional features do not always improve clustering.
explanations.c: New features usually affect distance calculations.
explanations.d: Poor feature choice may cause overlap.

explanations.a: Poor clustering produces higher DB scores.
explanations.b: DB score below 1 indicates acceptable clustering quality.
explanations.c: Good clustering usually has much lower DB values.
explanations.d: Excellent clustering has DB values close to 0.

explanations.a: CH score is not bounded to this range.
explanations.d: CH score starts from 0 and can increase infinitely.

explanations.a: K-means minimizes within-cluster sum of squares.
explanations.c: DB score is evaluated, not minimized by algorithm directly.
explanations.d: CH score is maximized, not minimized.

answers.c: Prevent features with large value to dominate and Equal contribution of each feature

explanations.a: Scaling does not directly reduce overfitting.
explanations.b: Evaluation metrics do not require scaling.
explanations.c: Scaling ensures fair distance contribution from all features.
explanations.d: Scaling does not reduce data size.

answers.c: Point where WCSS reduction slows down

explanations.a: WCSS keeps decreasing with increasing K.
explanations.b: CH score is a separate metric.
explanations.c: Elbow indicates diminishing returns with increasing clusters.

explanations.d: WCSS alone does not indicate overlap directly.

explanations.a: K is a hyperparameter in K-means.
explanations.c: Finding clusters does not define supervision.
explanations.d: Data type does not define learning type.
